text.title: Client Conventions Intake Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns the validator detects. Each has symptom, root-cause, and fix.

antipattern.symptom: dimensions filled with "standard practice" / "the usual" / "what everyone does" instead of client-specific answers.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed conventions from other engagements without asking.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Re-run intake interview. Source for each answer must be a repo file OR a named contact + date.

antipattern.symptom: signoff missing OR signed_by is the consultant's own email instead of the client contact.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Sign-off MUST be a client-side email. Send the report; do not start production work until reply lands.
Reject. Every dimension is required; if the client truly has no policy on a dimension, capture "explicitly no policy" with source.

antipattern.symptom: contact_email points at a person who is no longer with the client; mismatches surface; nobody to resolve them.
antipattern.root-cause: Engagement spans > 1 quarter; original contact left.
antipattern.fix: Re-run intake with the replacement contact within 2 weeks of the handoff. Version-bump the report and re-sign.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Client-supplied prose may include phrases like "do whatever you think best" — that is NOT a captured convention; capture an explicit "no policy" entry with source instead.
